2604.27253.title: AutoSurfer -- Teaching Web Agents through Comprehensive Surfing, Learning, and Modeling
2604.27253.paper_summary: Recent advances in multimodal large language models (LLMs) have revolutionized web agents that can automate complex tasks on websites. However, their accuracy remains limited by the scarcity of high-quality web trajectory training data. Existing automatic trajectory generation methods suffer from incomplete website coverage due to homepage-based task proposals or random-walk exploration. Such methods often result in hallucinated or ambiguous task synthesis that lead to incomplete and unreliable trajectory generation. Here, we present AutoSurfer, a comprehensive web trajectory generator that addresses these limitations through three key innovations. First, AutoSurfer employs a systematic breadth-first exploration strategy that maintains a queue of discovered pages and action traces, propagates knowledge across pages to avoid redundant exploration, and recursively expands multi-level graphical user interface elements - closely resembling how a human would learn a new website. Second, AutoSurfer leverages the exploration trajectory to guide task synthesis, reducing hallucinations by grounding complex tasks in actual navigation paths rather than isolated actions or page content alone. Third, AutoSurfer uses the same exploration trajectory as hints to steer a web agent toward more accurate and reliable trajectory refinement. Together, these innovations enable AutoSurfer to comprehensively cover a website's action space and generate data suitable for training website-specific LLMs. We evaluate AutoSurfer on the WebArena benchmark by fine-tuning Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct and demonstrate that it outperforms state-of-the-art methods - Explorer, OS-Genesis, and SynthAgent - achieving up to 24.23% overall task completion accuracy compared to 19.59% for the best prior method. Further, task diversity analysis demonstrates that AutoSurfer yields a more diverse distribution of synthesized tasks.
2604.27253.paper_summary_zh: 最近在多模態大型語言模型（LLMs）方面的進展已經徹底改變了能夠自動化網站上複雜任務的網頁代理。然而，它們的準確性仍然受到高品質網頁軌跡訓練數據稀缺的限制。現有的自動軌跡生成方法因為基於首頁的任務提議或隨機漫遊探索而遭受網站覆蓋不完整的問題。這些方法通常會導致虛幻或模糊的任務合成，從而導致不完整和不可靠的軌跡生成。在此，我們提出了AutoSurfer，一個全面的網頁軌跡生成器，通過三個關鍵創新來解決這些限制。首先，AutoSurfer採用系統性的廣度優先探索策略，維護已發現頁面和行動痕跡的隊列，跨頁面傳播知識以避免冗餘探索，並遞歸擴展多層次的圖形用戶界面元素——這與人類學習新網站的方式非常相似。其次，AutoSurfer利用探索軌跡來指導任務合成，通過將複雜任務根植於實際導航路徑而不是孤立的行動或頁面內容，來減少幻覺的產生。第三，AutoSurfer使用相同的探索軌跡作為提示，引導網頁代理朝向更準確和可靠的軌跡精煉。這些創新使AutoSurfer能夠全面覆蓋網站的行動空間，並生成適合於訓練網站特定LLMs的數據。我們在WebArena基準上評估AutoSurfer，通過微調Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct，並展示其超越最先進的方法——Explorer、OS-Genesis和SynthAgent，實現了高達24.23%的整體任務完成準確率，而最佳先前方法僅為19.59%。此外，任務多樣性分析顯示，AutoSurfer產生了更具多樣性的合成任務分佈。
2604.27253.author: Fazle Elahi Faisal et.al.
2604.27253.authors: Fazle Elahi Faisal, Qianhui Wu, Baolin Peng, Jianfeng Gao
